features.description: Create a standalone trusted-host Telegram polling Bot for personal home-watch use with /camera_clip <seconds>, /status, and /help; validate TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN and ALLOWED_CHAT_IDS, persist runtime_state.json with Telegram update offsets, restrict commands to authorized chats, parse ENABLE_CAMERA_CLIP_COMMAND and CAMERA_CLIP_COMMAND_JSON as a shell-disabled argv array template with {seconds} and {output}, record clips for integer durations from 1 through 10, reject disabled or malformed capture config, reject concurrent capture, enforce capture timeout, send successful clips through Telegram sendVideo, delete temporary media after success or failure, avoid Codex and repository workflow commands, document setup and BotFather commands, and add unit, harness, contract, smoke, and init coverage using fake capture and fake Telegram transport without real camera or network requirements.

Add /photo for authorized chats to capture one still image and send it through Telegram sendPhoto; keep the command disabled unless explicitly enabled, parse a shell-disabled argv array template containing {output}, let the configured local capture argv and output path determine photo extension, format, and quality without Bot-side transcoding by default, reject disabled or malformed config before capture, reject concurrent media captures, enforce bounded timeout, avoid sendPhoto on failed or empty output, delete temporary photo files after send success or failure, and cover behavior with fake capture and fake Telegram transport tests.

Add /schedule_photo HH:MM for authorized chats to schedule one daily still photo capture at a 24-hour server-local time using the Mac's local timezone rather than Telegram or user-local timezone; persist the schedule in runtime state across Bot restarts, allow only one active daily schedule unless later requirements change this, reject a new schedule when one already exists and tell the user to run /cancel_schedule first, require valid enabled photo capture configuration, reuse /photo timeout cleanup and send behavior, prevent overlap with active captures, report configured local time and timezone context, and avoid hidden continuous monitoring beyond the explicit daily trigger.

Add /cancel_schedule for authorized chats to cancel the active daily photo schedule; clear persisted schedule state, behave idempotently when no schedule exists, report whether a schedule was cancelled or absent, and do not interrupt already-started media capture unless a later requirement explicitly requests in-flight cancellation.

Add cautious-use /sound_alarm <seconds> for authorized chats to play a local audible alert on the Mac for a bounded duration; keep it disabled unless explicitly enabled, accept only integer seconds from 1 through 30, do not require two-step confirmation beyond authorized-chat access, run a configured argv array template or verified built-in platform command without unbounded shell strings, enforce timeout and cleanup, reject concurrent alarms, document cautious use in help/status surfaces, and ensure default tests never play real audio.

Enhance /status for authorized chats to report bounded host and Bot state including Bot uptime, camera clip config enabled/valid state, photo config enabled/valid state, active capture or alarm state, Mac battery level when available, Mac power source or charging state when available, private local network IP information without secrets and without public-IP lookup by default, remaining disk space, and current response timestamp for online and latency confirmation; host telemetry must degrade gracefully with bounded unavailable values and must not block polling indefinitely.

Add /logs for authorized chats to return only recent Bot-owned runtime errors; maintain a bounded persisted local error log on the server, redact secrets, exclude video/photo content and sensitive media data, avoid returning general host logs, bound Telegram output length, and report a clear empty state when no recent errors are available.

Add /camera_test for authorized chats to run a short ffmpeg dry probe and return the last bounded stderr lines for diagnosing camera permission, device index, and timeout problems; keep it disabled unless explicitly enabled, use a configured argv array or verified ffmpeg argv without shell strings, enforce short timeout, redact secrets, never include media content, verify real ffmpeg behavior or document why direct verification was not possible, and cover default behavior with fake probe output without requiring a real camera.
